name: Enterprise Deal Strategy
description: Team strategizes on a $500K enterprise deal, simulating buyer committee dynamics and multi-threaded engagement approaches.
seed_text:
Intercom is pursuing a $500K ARR enterprise deal with a Fortune 500 financial services company (codename: 'Project Atlas'). The prospect currently uses Zendesk Enterprise ($380K/year) and is evaluating Intercom and Freshdesk as replacements during their contract renewal window, which closes in 8 weeks.

Buyer committee mapped so far:
- Executive Sponsor: SVP of Customer Experience — met once, expressed interest in AI-first support but concerned about migration risk
- Champion: Director of Support Operations — strong advocate, ran a 30-day Fin pilot with 52% resolution rate, but lacks budget authority
- Technical Evaluator: VP of Engineering — concerned about API compatibility with their custom Salesforce integration and SSO requirements
- Blocker: CFO — has a mandate to cut vendor spend 15% across the board; views any solution above Zendesk's current cost as a non-starter
- End Users: 200+ support agents — change-resistant, heavy Zendesk macro users, worried about learning curve

Deal dynamics:
1. Zendesk has offered a 25% renewal discount ($285K/year) plus free AI add-on for 12 months to retain
2. Freshdesk is bidding at $210K/year but lacks enterprise security certifications the prospect requires
3. The Champion is pushing for Intercom but needs a business case showing 18-month ROI to get CFO approval
4. Legal has flagged Intercom's data processing agreement — they need EU data residency for 40% of their customer base
5. The prospect's board meets in 6 weeks and the SVP wants to present a vendor recommendation

The simulation should model the buying committee dynamics, test different deal strategies (pricing, packaging, executive alignment), and predict which approach maximizes win probability while protecting deal value.
expected_outputs: Buyer committee influence map and engagement strategy
Win probability score under different pricing scenarios
Competitive displacement playbook vs Zendesk renewal offer
ROI business case framework for CFO objection handling
Deal timeline risk assessment and acceleration tactics
